hero.title: STAR WARS: Galactic Racer Wiki
hero.description: Race through the Outer Rim in a high-stakes Star Wars racing adventure with podracers, landspeeders, speeder bikes, custom builds, solo campaign, arcade events, and cross-platform multiplayer.

swgrReleaseDatePlatforms.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Release Date, Platforms, and Store Availability
swgrReleaseDatePlatforms.intro: Star Wars Galactic Racer launches worldwide on October 6, 2026 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC, developed by Fuse Games and published by Secret Mode.

items.label: Launch Date
items.detail: Worldwide launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

items.label: Platforms
items.detail: PC listings are available through Steam and the Epic Games Store.

items.label: Developer
items.detail: Fuse Games is listed as the developer on the major storefronts.

items.label: Publisher
items.detail: Secret Mode is listed as the publisher across all storefronts.

items.label: Store Availability
items.detail: Standard and Deluxe digital editions are listed on Steam, PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, and Epic Games Store.

items.label: Standard Edition Price
items.detail: The Standard Edition is listed at $59.99 across all storefronts.

swgrEditionsAndBonuses.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Editions and Pre-Order Bonuses
swgrEditionsAndBonuses.intro: Compare the Standard, Deluxe, and Collector's Edition bundles. Every pre-order includes the bonus repulsorcraft livery and Player Banner, while Deluxe and Collector's Edition add vehicles, Arcade Events, and art content.

editions.format: Digital edition on Steam, PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, and Epic Games Store
editions.contentFocus: Base game plus launch pre-order cosmetics

editions.format: Digital edition on Steam, Xbox Store, and Epic Games Store; Deluxe content also available as a Deluxe Upgrade add-on
editions.contentFocus: Digital extras, exclusive vehicles, Arcade trials, and art content

editions.format: Physical edition through the official site
editions.contentFocus: Full Deluxe content plus physical collectibles

swgrTrailerAndNewsTimeline.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Trailers and Official News Timeline
swgrTrailerAndNewsTimeline.intro: Follow the reveal, gameplay, release date, story trailer, and hands-on preview beats in one clean timeline, built around official Star Wars, official video, and Xbox Wire coverage.

entries.summary: The reveal introduced Star Wars Galactic Racer as a new high-stakes racing game set in the lawless Outer Rim, centered on Shade, the Galactic League, Jakku, and early racing footage.
The gameplay trailer showed racing across Sentinel One, Jakku, Ando Prime, and Lantaana, with landspeeders, skim speeders, speeder bikes, podracers, rival pilots, parts, tools, and upgrades.
The release date update confirmed the October 6, 2026 launch for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC, with pre-orders open for the Standard, Deluxe, and Collector's Edition.
The Summer Game Fest story trailer focused on Shade, Kestar Bool, Hibi, Sebulba, the Derven Acos arena, single-player progression, and multiplayer podracing beyond the Mos Espa Circuit.
Xbox Wire detailed the runs-based Galactic Tour structure, three chassis choices, upgrade categories, League Entry Token risk, Arcade mode, paddock downtime, and multiple racing worlds.

swgrGameplayModes.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Gameplay Modes, Campaign, and Multiplayer
swgrGameplayModes.intro: Star Wars Galactic Racer mixes a runs-based solo campaign with Scenario and Arcade modes, PvP grudge matches, and online multiplayer. Storefronts list online support for up to 12 players, with cross-platform support shown on Xbox and Epic.

modes.description: A story-driven solo campaign where Shade climbs the Galactic League through a runs-based Galactic Tour with branching paths, escalating events, rewards, and upgrades.
Scenario mode is listed as one of the core modes alongside Campaign and Arcade, giving the game a focused challenge format outside the main tour structure.
Arcade mode presents curated events using specific racers, speeders, and locations across the Galactic League. Deluxe content also adds three Arcade trials designed for the Kor Sarun repulsorcraft.
keyFeatures: Tatooine track featured in hands-on preview

modes.description: PvP grudge matches are competitive races where players battle rivals and reputation is part of the stakes, positioned as strategic multiplayer competition.
Online multiplayer is listed on console storefronts, with Xbox cross-platform multiplayer and Epic Games Store cross-platform support shown in the store capabilities.
keyFeatures: PS Plus required for online play on PS5

swgrBeginnerGuide.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Beginner Guide
swgrBeginnerGuide.intro: Learn the first-play flow for Star Wars Galactic Racer Campaign runs, early chassis choices, crashes, takedowns, upgrades, and core race types.

steps.title: Start with Campaign mode
steps.summary: Campaign mode follows Shade, a racer entering the Galactic League to challenge Kestar Bool. The solo campaign uses Galactic Tours with branching event paths, escalating races, rewards, and rebuild moments between attempts.
steps.tip: Treat the campaign as the main learning path before jumping into harder arcade trials or PvP grudge matches.

steps.title: Pick an early chassis that matches your control style
steps.summary: Hands-on previews highlight three early chassis choices: landspeeder, speeder bike, and skim speeder. Each chassis changes handling, speed, cornering, and the way upgrades matter during a run.
mechanics: Speeder Bike: fast in a straight line
steps.tip: Use the landspeeder first when learning collisions, drifting, and upgrade timing, then move to the speeder bike or skim speeder once corner control feels reliable.

steps.title: Learn starts, boosts, and overheating
steps.summary: Race starts include timing the accelerator and a quick engine-and-shield input sequence for a launch boost. During races, each speeder uses an Afterburner for repeatable boosts and a Ramjet for high-risk bursts that can help with takedowns but can overheat.
steps.tip: Save Ramjet use for straight sections or takedown attempts instead of boosting into corners.

steps.title: Race aggressively, but do not crash out
steps.summary: Star Wars Galactic Racer rewards contact racing. Players can slam, shunt, and takedown rivals across circuits, but crashes and poor placements can end a tour.
steps.tip: Use takedowns to create space, not as a substitute for racing lines.

steps.title: Spend upgrades between races
steps.summary: Between events, players can improve speeder performance and apply rewards from route choices. Known upgrade areas include Afterburner, Battery, Cornering, Ramjet, and chassis traits such as Drift, Kinetic Burst, and Knife Edge.
steps.tip: Prioritize Cornering and Afterburner while learning; pure top speed matters less than clean corner exits.

steps.title: Understand tour failure and recovery
steps.summary: A League Entry Token is needed to compete in a tour. Crashing too many times or failing to place in the top three can revoke the Token and end the tour, forcing a fresh attempt while some progress such as Galactic Credits can carry forward.
steps.tip: Finish safely before chasing perfect takedown chains; surviving the tour matters more than one flashy corner.

steps.title: Use Arcade and other race types for practice
steps.summary: The game includes Campaign, Scenario, and Arcade modes. Previewed race formats include standard races, time trials, elimination events, 1v1 events, and podracing-focused events with higher speed and tighter danger windows.
steps.tip: Use Arcade to practice specific vehicles and locations before committing a League Entry Token in the campaign.

swgrVehiclesAndCustomization.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Vehicles and Customization
swgrVehiclesAndCustomization.intro: Compare the confirmed repulsorcraft classes, handling styles, upgrades, liveries, Deluxe vehicles, and known vehicle abilities across Star Wars Galactic Racer solo and multiplayer modes.

vehicles.handling: Weightier handling with a strong drifting identity.
vehicles.useCase: Learning racing lines, managing contact, and surviving aggressive races.

vehicles.handling: Strong straight-line speed with more demanding corner control.
vehicles.useCase: Fast sections, risky overtakes, and players who can control top speed into corners.

vehicles.handling: Built around hard banking turns and a more technical feel.
vehicles.useCase: Technical handling, sharp direction changes, and advanced route control.

vehicles.handling: High-speed racing focus, with podracing positioned as a major showcase mode.
vehicles.useCase: High-speed events, iconic Star Wars racing moments, and Tatooine-style track mastery.

vehicles.handling: Three exclusive Kor Sarun repulsorcraft tied to Deluxe content.
vehicles.useCase: Players who want extra vehicles, themed Arcade trials, and Deluxe cosmetic content.

vehicles.availability: Base game, pre-order bonuses, and Deluxe content
vehicles.handling: Cosmetic identity for repulsorcraft and multiplayer profile presentation.
vehicles.useCase: Personalize vehicles and multiplayer identity without changing the driving class.

vehicles.handling: Race-to-race performance tuning tied to event rewards and progression.
vehicles.useCase: Build a vehicle for safer cornering, stronger boosts, harder takedowns, or class-specific strengths.

swgrTracksAndPlanets.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Tracks and Planets
swgrTracksAndPlanets.intro: Explore the confirmed planets, circuits, arenas, terrain themes, and trailer-featured racing locations across the Star Wars Galactic Racer Galactic League.

locations.name: Jakku
locations.notes: Jakku is presented as an unsanctioned racing location and a key early showcase world for the Galactic League.
locations.hazards: Dust, wreckage-heavy scenery, and Outer Rim scrapyard energy.

locations.name: Tatooine
locations.environment: Desert racing world tied to classic Star Wars podracing
locations.notes: Tatooine includes podracing action and the Mos Espa Circuit connection, with Sebulba featured in previewed Arcade racing.
locations.hazards: High-speed podracing, shortcuts, narrow routes, caves, and crash-heavy danger.

locations.name: Mos Espa Circuit
locations.notes: A famous Tatooine track inspired by Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace, highlighted in hands-on coverage.
locations.hazards: Podracing speed, close track control, and cinematic Star Wars racing nostalgia.

locations.name: Ando Prime
locations.notes: Ando Prime appears as one of the distinct racing worlds, with previews emphasizing its frozen terrain.
locations.hazards: Ice, slippery racing pressure, and harsh cold-world visibility.

locations.name: Lantaana
locations.notes: Lantaana is introduced as a verdant planet and one of the campaign tour racing worlds.
locations.hazards: Dense jungle scenery and tropical track atmosphere.

locations.name: Sentinel I
locations.notes: Sentinel I is shown in official materials as a distinct Galactic League racing planet.
locations.hazards: Volcanic terrain and hostile track scenery.

locations.name: Derven Acos Arena
locations.notes: The story trailer reveals the Derven Acos arena as a new location tied to single-player footage and multiplayer podracing coverage.
locations.hazards: Arena racing presentation and League spectacle.

swgrSystemRequirements.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer PC System Requirements
swgrSystemRequirements.intro: The Star Wars Galactic Racer PC requirements list Windows 10, a mid-range CPU, 12 GB RAM, an RTX 2060-class GPU, 50 GB SSD storage, and gamepad support.

specs.notes: Listed on Steam and the Epic Games Store.

specs.minimum: Nvidia RTX 2060 / Intel Arc A580 / AMD Radeon RX 5600 XT 6GB
specs.notes: Minimum GPU class listed by Steam and Epic.
An SSD is required for installation.
Epic lists Gamepad as an additional input device.

specs.minimum: Single-player, Online PvP, Cross-Platform Multiplayer, Family Sharing
specs.notes: Steam also lists Denuvo Anti-Tamper with a 5-machine activation limit.

specs.minimum: English full audio plus 14 interface and subtitle languages
specs.notes: French, Italian, German, Spanish, Arabic, Japanese, Korean, Polish, Portuguese, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and Turkish.

specs.minimum: PS5 and Xbox Series X|S available
specs.notes: PS Store lists 1 player, optional online up to 12 with PS Plus, and Remote Play. Xbox Store lists single player, online multiplayer 2-12, and cross-platform multiplayer.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Star Wars Galactic Racer

questions.question: What is Star Wars Galactic Racer?
questions.answer: Star Wars Galactic Racer is a runs-based Outer Rim racing game with a story-driven campaign, Arcade events, PvP grudge matches, and online multiplayer for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

questions.question: When does Star Wars Galactic Racer release?
questions.answer: Star Wars Galactic Racer launches worldwide on October 6, 2026 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

questions.question: What platforms is Star Wars Galactic Racer on?
questions.answer: Star Wars Galactic Racer is available on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC through Steam and the Epic Games Store.

questions.question: Which vehicles can you race in Star Wars Galactic Racer?
questions.answer: Star Wars Galactic Racer includes landspeeders, speeder bikes, skim speeders, and podracers, with the Deluxe Edition adding three exclusive Kor Sarun repulsorcraft.

questions.question: Does Star Wars Galactic Racer have multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Star Wars Galactic Racer lists online multiplayer for up to 12 players on PS5 and 2-12 on Xbox, with cross-platform support shown on Xbox and the Epic Games Store.

questions.question: What are the PC requirements for Star Wars Galactic Racer?
questions.answer: Star Wars Galactic Racer lists Windows 10, an Intel Core i5-8400 or AMD Ryzen 5 2600, 12 GB RAM, an RTX 2060-class GPU, and 50 GB SSD storage.
